code_gaps.description: Scene deserialization fails for PrototypeBoard and GameScene (GameController node) due to 'getComponent: Type must be non-nil' (2 errors). The @property(Label) decorators on PrototypeBoard.comboLabel and PrototypeBoard.clearedLabel resolve to undefined during CC3 scene JSON deserialization, preventing the Board node from being created from the scene file. As a result, neither the Prototype nor the GameScene loads its primary game component from the scene file. The PrototypeBoard works correctly when added programmatically. Likely cause: the scene .json stores component type references by UUID and the Label type import at deserialization time does not match the registered cc.Label class reference.

PrototypeBoard HUD bootstrap (_bootstrapHUD) correctly creates the HUD node hierarchy with ComboLabel, ClearedLabel, StepsLabel and Reset button when @property refs are not wired. However, the comboLabel/clearedLabel/stepsLabel refs on the PrototypeBoard instance are not set to the dynamically-created labels during bootstrap, causing updateLabels() calls to silently no-op on labels. The HUD nodes exist in the scene tree but are not reachable via pb.comboLabel etc. This is a minor display-only issue: the HUD displays initial values ('COMBO × 0 (×1.0)', 'Cleared: 0', 'Steps: 0') set during start() but does not update on subsequent matches.

The Playwright screenshot capture shows the CC3 WebGL canvas as black. This is a known limitation of WebGL/Playwright screenshot capture on macOS — Playwright's CSS-based screenshot does not capture the WebGL framebuffer. The game is visually rendering (180 triangles, 60 FPS, Draw call=1 confirmed in FPS overlay stats) but cannot be verified visually via screenshots. This is a test infrastructure gap, not a game code gap.

notes: Core gameplay loop is functionally verified via CC3 runtime JS evaluation: tile matching (BFS path-finding with ≤2 turns), combo system (2000ms window, incremental combo counter), tile vanish animation, and gravity/column-drop after match all work correctly. The primary blocker is the scene deserialization error (E2E-01) which prevents the game from loading from the scene file — the PrototypeBoard must be instantiated programmatically. The GameScene (GameController) has the same deserialization issue and additionally requires AudioManager, LevelLoader, and level config files to be functional. The move counter (Steps) is implemented in PrototypeBoard as 'cleared/2 | 0' and updates via updateLabels(). The page loads at 60 FPS with full CC3 engine stack (physics, WebGL, audio) initialized.
